titlestmt.titleproper: The Yale School of Music D.M.A. Papers
Yale School of Music D.M.A. Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Jean Elizabeth Roberts; revised by Richard Boursy

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday August 21, 2020 at 00:00
music.mss.0013.xml converted for compliance with Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines with music-migrate-02.xsl (yn2007-08-24).
music.mss.0013.xml converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 with v1to02.xsl (yn2007-03-20).
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.
Added Dmitri Atapine (cello, 2009), and added scrapbook for Craig Hella Johnson (Box 177).
Corrected box number for Atapine; it should be 162, not 163.

New accessions: portfolios for 2012-2015, and evaluations (boxed separately) for 2004-2015. This accession contained evaluations--but no dossiers!--for Daniel Tzer Wei Koh and Bradley Naylor, so their names are not included in the regular listings.

did.unittitle: The Yale School of Music D.M.A. Papers
did.abstract: Dossiers documenting the professional activities of successful candidates for the degree of Doctor of Musical Arts in the Yale School of Music

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Dossiers are made available for research in the Music Library upon application to a Music Library staff member. In some instances, permission to access an individual dossier may also require permission from the subject. Because the correspondence files contain confidential academic evaluations, they will remain sealed until 75 years after the D.M.A. was awarded.
Original computer files may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult access copies.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Yale School of Music Papers are the physical property of the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University. Copyrights belong to the composers and authors, or their legal heirs and assigns.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: MSS 13, The Yale School of Music D.M.A. Papers in the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Materials are arranged chronologically by year of degree, and alphabetically by last name therein.
Original order has been maintained for born-digital materials. No further arrangement work has been done.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Yale School of Music D.M.A. Papers document the professional activities of successful candidates for the degree of Doctor of Musical Arts. The Papers cover a degree candidate's activities between receipt of the Master of Musical Arts degree and return for the D.M.A. degree. Included in the Papers are scrapbooks, correspondence, programs, reviews, sound recordings, and printed books and musical scores.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Doctor of Musical Arts degree program of the Yale School of Music was approved in 1968; the first degrees were awarded in 1973. As a prerequisite, candidates must have received the Master of Musical Arts degree from Yale, and then spend three to five years demonstrating their qualifications through distinguished achievement in their profession. Upon applying for the D.M.A. degree, the candidates submit a dossier of evidence demonstrating their professional competence and accomplishments. This evidence may include programs, brochures, reviews, compositions, recordings, publications, and other materials. Candidates are also required to give a recital and undergo an oral examination. Each year, the School of Music sends dossiers from successful candidates to the Library to become part of the D.M.A. Papers.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The original file structure for the computer files in this collection has been maintained.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: Dossiers created in and after 2015 are comprised of computer files, predominately document files (PDFs), audio files (MP4s), and image files (Jpegs).

subject.part: Music -- Instruction and study -- United States

did.unittitle: Composition tapes: three cassettes, one reel
Composition by Peter Dickson Lopez, written for Jeffrey Cox
Trio for soprano, viola, and clarinet
Buk San Mo Ang Aming Maa Labi (score and tape)

unittitle.emph: Il Est Né. Le Divin Enfant
Romansa : La Esposa de Don Garcia
Concerto For Cello And Chamber Orchestra

did.unittitle: Ferlendis, G. Sonata for English Horn
Bach, J.C. Quartet in G Major

unittitle.emph: Beams Under The Action Of A Moving Rhythm

did.unittitle: A Fire's Bright Song For Orchestra

unittitle.emph: Be not the slave of words

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Computer files in this series were imaged or captured by Gilmore Music Library staff in 2015 for preservation and access. Additional information about actions taken may be available at the repository.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact the Music Library at MusicLibrary@yale.edu to request access.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Computer files in this series were imaged or captured by Gilmore Music Library staff in 2015 for preservation and access. Additional information about actions taken may be available at the repository.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dates for the computer files included in the series were determined by the year they were transferred to the Gilmore Music Library by the Yale School of Music.
